symptom: drone runs shell_exec / undertow / file_read for 8+ iterations in a row after a message_chat delivery; the existing 'VERIFICATION STALL' advisory fires but the drone keeps checking without making changes
root_cause: the stall detector emitted a prompt-level nudge and returned — the drone's next-iter schema still included all read-only tools so nothing changed
fix_approach: when the stall condition fires (4+ consecutive read-only tools + prior message_chat + iter > 5), set _loop_forced_tool='message_result'. The next iter's drone schema is locked to message_result only (same mechanism the loop_guard.detected branch uses). Force is cleared on successful message_result emit via the existing _pf satisfaction check at L3783.
principle: v5 — the old note asked; the new force enforces. Re-uses the existing _loop_forced_tool plumbing so no new surface to maintain.

required_fragments_absent: VERIFICATION STALL: You've been checking the build for 8+

desc: force respects the existing _pf satisfaction / clear mechanism
required_fragments: if _pf and not result.is_error and tool_call.name in (_pf, "message_result"):

desc: force is picked up by the schema gate at top of _step
